No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF WILLIE AUSTIN ON BEHALF OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA AS A PRIVATE ATTORNEY GENERAL's Unopposed Motion For Dismissal Of Action Without Prejudice.

Plaintiff's unopposed Motion for Dismissal of Action Without Prejudice is granted.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Rochelle C. East; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RCE)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay
Off Calendar Jun-11-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Piya Mukherjee, Esq. (818-939-5165; piya@bamlawca.com) of Blumenthal Noredrehaugh Bhowmik De Blouw LLP for Plaintiff.
Cory D. Catignani. Esq. (949-526-7900; cdcatignani@vorys.com) of Vorys, Sater, Seymour and Pease LLP for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SAFELITE FULFILLMENT, INC.'s Motion To Stay:

Defendant Safelite Fulfillment Inc.'s motion to stay this case is granted. Plaintiff Willie Austin, Jr. has a first-filed putative class action pending in federal court a block away. As Austin states, his private attorneys general case in this court alleges "the same violations of the Labor Code" by the same defendant that his federal case alleges. Austin notes that "procedural mechanisms" differ in his two cases. However, that does not address the key points: It would be duplicative, inefficient and harassing to litigate the same claims against the same defendant in two courts simultaneously. (See, e.g., Caiafa Prof. Law Corp. v. State Farm Fire & Cas. Co. (1993) 15 Cal.App.4th 800, 804.) Austin cites no case denying a stay in circumstances like ours.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
